sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a Cherethite, an executioner (see the root כָּרַת No. 1 , 1Sa 5:4), only found in the phrase הַכְּרֵתִי וְהַפְּלֵתִי 2Sa 8:18 15:18 20:7 , 23 2Sa 20:23 (collect.) executioners and runners, a name borne by the guards of king David, whose office it was both to inflict capital punishments, and to convey the king’s mandates as quickly as possible to those who held places of government (compare כָּרִי, טַבָּח, אִגֶּרֶת). See 1Ki 2:25, 36 1Ki 2:36, 46 1Ki 2:46comp. Dan 2:14.

(2) [Cherethites], a Gentile name, i.q. Philistine, especially used of the inhabitants of the southern part of Philistia, 1Sa 30:14 Eze 25:16 Zep 2:5. LXX. and the Syr. render it Cretans, whence by a comparison with the passages, Amo 9:7 Jer 47:4 Deu 2:23, it is not amiss to conjecture that the Philistines had their origin from Crete, and that כַּפְתֹּר signifies that island; see that word. Others suppose כְּרֵתִי to be so called from כָּרַת to be expelled from one’s country ( Zec 14:2), so that it would be the same as Ἀλλόφυλος, by which word פְּלִשְׁתִּי is rendered by the LXX. [in some parts of the O.T.]
